core_claim: LoRA, which freezes pre-trained weights and injects trainable low-rank decomposition matrices, performs on-par or better than full fine-tuning on RoBERTa, DeBERTa, GPT-2, and GPT-3 while reducing trainable parameters by up to 10,000x and GPU memory by 3x, with no additional inference latency.

datasets.name: GLUE Benchmark
datasets.preprocessing: Sequence length 128 for RoBERTa adapter comparison setup, 512 for RoBERTa base LoRA, 256 for DeBERTa XXL. Includes MNLI, SST-2, MRPC, CoLA, QNLI, QQP, RTE, STS-B.

datasets.name: E2E NLG Challenge
datasets.preprocessing: Restaurant domain data-to-text. Each sample consists of slot-value pairs and natural language reference text. Released under Creative Commons BY-NC-SA 4.0.

datasets.name: WebNLG
datasets.preprocessing: Input represented as SUBJECT | PROPERTY | OBJECT triples. 14 categories, 9 seen during training, 5 unseen. Evaluation on Seen (S), Unseen (U), and All (A).

datasets.name: DART
datasets.preprocessing: Input structured as ENTITY | RELATION | ENTITY triples. Open-domain data-to-text. Released under MIT license.

datasets.name: WikiSQL
datasets.preprocessing: Context encoded as {table schema, query}, target as {SQL}. Sequence length 384 for GPT-3. Released under BSD 3-Clause License.

datasets.name: SAMSum
datasets.preprocessing: Staged chat conversations with abstractive summaries. Context is newline-concatenated utterances. Sequence length 2048 for GPT-3. Released under Creative Commons BY-NC-ND 4.0.

datasets.name: MNLI
datasets.preprocessing: Natural language inference. Sequence length 768 for GPT-3.

model.architecture: LoRA applied to pre-trained Transformer models (RoBERTa base/large, DeBERTa XXL, GPT-2 Medium/Large, GPT-3 175B). LoRA injects trainable rank decomposition matrices B*A in parallel to frozen weight matrices W_q and W_v in self-attention. A initialized with random Gaussian, B initialized to zero. Output scaled by alpha/r.

metrics.name: BLEU
metrics.conditions: GPT-2 Medium, LoRA r=4 on W_q and W_v, 0.35M trainable parameters

metrics.name: NIST
metrics.conditions: GPT-2 Medium, LoRA r=4 on W_q and W_v, 0.35M trainable parameters

metrics.name: MET
metrics.conditions: GPT-2 Medium, LoRA r=4 on W_q and W_v, 0.35M trainable parameters

metrics.name: ROUGE-L
metrics.conditions: GPT-2 Medium, LoRA r=4 on W_q and W_v, 0.35M trainable parameters

metrics.name: CIDEr
metrics.conditions: GPT-2 Medium, LoRA r=4 on W_q and W_v, 0.35M trainable parameters

metrics.name: Avg. GLUE score
metrics.conditions: RoBERTa base, LoRA r=8 on W_q and W_v, 0.3M trainable parameters

metrics.name: Avg. GLUE score
metrics.conditions: RoBERTa large, LoRA r=8 on W_q and W_v, 0.8M trainable parameters

metrics.name: Avg. GLUE score
metrics.conditions: DeBERTa XXL, LoRA r=8 on W_q and W_v, 4.7M trainable parameters

ablations.name: weight_type_selection
ablations.description: Compares applying LoRA to different subsets of attention weight matrices (W_q, W_k, W_v, W_o and combinations) on GPT-3 175B. Adapting both W_q and W_v yields the best result with r=4; putting all parameters into a single matrix type (e.g., W_q only) performs significantly worse.

ablations.name: rank_r_effect
ablations.description: Tests LoRA rank r from 1 to 64 on GPT-3 175B for WikiSQL and MultiNLI. LoRA performs competitively even with very small r (r=1 or r=2), suggesting the update matrix has very low intrinsic rank. Performance does not improve monotonically with higher r.

ablations.name: rank_r_effect_gpt2
ablations.description: Tests LoRA rank r from 1 to 1024 on GPT-2 Medium for E2E NLG Challenge. Optimal rank is between 4 and 16 depending on metric. Very high ranks do not improve performance.

ablations.name: lora_plus_prefix_tuning
ablations.description: Combines LoRA with prefix-embedding tuning (LoRA+PE) and prefix-layer tuning (LoRA+PL) on GPT-3. LoRA+PE significantly outperforms both methods alone on WikiSQL, showing orthogonality. LoRA+PL performs slightly worse than LoRA alone due to learning rate sensitivity.

ablations.name: low_data_regime
ablations.description: Tests adaptation methods on MNLI with 100, 1k, 10k, and full training examples. LoRA achieves 63.8% on MNLI-100 vs 60.2% for fine-tuning and 37.6% for PrefixEmbed. Prefix-based approaches are not suitable for low-data tasks on GPT-3.

checkpoints.name: RoBERTa base LoRA checkpoint
checkpoints.what_it_is: Trained LoRA adapter weights for RoBERTa base on GLUE tasks

checkpoints.name: RoBERTa large LoRA checkpoint
checkpoints.what_it_is: Trained LoRA adapter weights for RoBERTa large on GLUE tasks

checkpoints.name: DeBERTa XXL LoRA checkpoint
checkpoints.what_it_is: Trained LoRA adapter weights for DeBERTa XXL on GLUE tasks

checkpoints.name: GPT-2 LoRA checkpoint
checkpoints.what_it_is: Trained LoRA adapter weights for GPT-2 on E2E NLG Challenge, DART, and WebNLG

eval_scripts.purpose: Beam search decoding and evaluation for GPT-2 LoRA on NLG tasks (E2E, DART, WebNLG)
Evaluation metrics computation for NLG tasks

reproducibility_notes: Authors release a PyTorch package (loralib) for integrating LoRA, plus implementations and model checkpoints for RoBERTa, DeBERTa, and GPT-2 at https://github.com/microsoft/LoRA. The repo contains examples/NLG/ with training and evaluation scripts, and a download script for pretrained checkpoints. All experiments use NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs. Results for RoBERTa and DeBERTa report median over 5 random seeds; GPT-2 reports mean over 3 random seeds. GPT-3 experiments report typical standard deviation over random seeds.
compute_feasibility_note: Released checkpoints for GPT-2 Medium LoRA on E2E NLG support a main claim (BLEU 70.4) and can be evaluated on a single GPU or even CPU in minutes, since the base model is only ~350M parameters (~1.4GB in FP32) and LoRA adds negligible overhead. For RoBERTa/DeBERTa GLUE claims, released checkpoints also enable eval-only reproduction. Training from scratch is also feasible on a single V100 GPU within hours for GPT-2 experiments.
